title: rosa tomentosa subsp. foetida
intro: Rosa tomentosa subsp. foetida, also known as the stinking rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa tomentosa plant, native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, characterized by its strong, unpleasant odor and medicinal properties. This plant has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can help in treating various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Rosa tomentosa subsp. foetida include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and providing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include essential oils, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
